description: Identify gradual memory retention under stable load.

messages.content: Return only JSON with category, severity, and recommended_action.

During 50 minutes of constant traffic, worker RSS climbs monotonically from 700 MiB to 3.1 GiB. Queue depth and CPU stay flat, and completed jobs do not release memory. Choose a category from memory_leak, dependency_failure, network_timeout, configuration_error, resource_exhaustion, unknown; choose a safe allowed action.

description: Identify an unavailable required dependency.

messages.content: Return only JSON with category, severity, and recommended_action.

All workers report connection refused from the configured cache endpoint. Local CPU, memory, and disk are normal, and DNS resolves to the expected address. Classify using the provided reliability categories and choose one allowed recovery action.

description: Identify repeated upstream network timeouts.

messages.content: Return only JSON with category, severity, and recommended_action.

Requests to a healthy remote API resolve DNS and establish TCP, but 38% exceed the 10-second response deadline. Other dependencies respond normally and host resources are below 50%. Classify the incident.

description: Identify a deployment configuration mismatch.

messages.content: Return only JSON with category, severity, and recommended_action.

Immediately after deployment, every worker rejects startup because REQUIRED_REGION is absent. The prior release starts with the same binaries when that setting is present. Classify the incident and choose a bounded action.

description: Identify worker exhaustion caused by a full bounded queue.

messages.content: Return only JSON with category, severity, and recommended_action.
The bounded queue is at 10,000 of 10,000 entries, all 16 worker slots are busy, and new requests are rejected. Dependencies are healthy and configuration is unchanged. Classify the incident.

description: Retry a single idempotent transient request.

messages.content: Return only JSON with action and rationale.
An idempotent health metadata request timed out once, the endpoint is healthy now, and two retries remain. Select one: retry, restart, quarantine, operator_review, take_no_action.

description: Restart a wedged disposable worker after health-check failure.

messages.content: Return only JSON with action and rationale.
A disposable stateless worker has failed its local health check for ten minutes. Its in-flight work is safely checkpointed and peer workers are healthy. Select one allowed action.

description: Quarantine a repeatedly malformed input item.

messages.content: Return only JSON with action and rationale.
One input item fails deterministic schema validation on every retry while other items process normally. Preserve it for inspection and prevent it from blocking the queue. Select one allowed action.

description: Escalate an ambiguous certificate identity mismatch.

messages.content: Return only JSON with action and rationale.

A new certificate is valid and unexpired but names a service identity not present in the approved change record. Automated retry and restart cannot resolve identity authorization. Select one allowed action.

description: Take no action after a self-resolved informational event.

messages.content: Return only JSON with action and rationale.
A single informational probe was delayed for 80 ms, remained within its 500 ms SLO, and all subsequent probes are normal. No errors or user impact occurred. Select one allowed action.

description: Summarize cache connection refusal evidence.

messages.content: Return only JSON with root_cause, evidence, uncertainty, and recommended_action.
09:10 cache lookup begins; 09:10 cache:6379 connection refused three times; 09:11 CPU 21%, memory 38%; 09:11 request fails. Summarize only supported evidence.

description: Summarize an invalid configuration value.

messages.content: Return only JSON with root_cause, evidence, uncertainty, and recommended_action.
14:02 startup reads WORKER_COUNT=zero; 14:02 parser reports expected positive integer; 14:02 process exits before opening a socket. Summarize defensively.

description: Summarize a remote response timeout.

messages.content: Return only JSON with root_cause, evidence, uncertainty, and recommended_action.
16:20 DNS succeeds; 16:20 TCP connects in 12 ms; 16:30 upstream response deadline exceeded; local CPU 16% and free memory 6 GiB. Summarize what is known and uncertain.

description: Summarize bounded disk exhaustion.

messages.content: Return only JSON with root_cause, evidence, uncertainty, and recommended_action.
18:00 disk usage 100%; 18:00 append fails with no space left on device; 18:01 CPU 9%; 18:01 service enters read-only safety mode. Summarize without proposing deletion.

description: Preserve uncertainty when evidence is insufficient.

messages.content: Return only JSON with root_cause, evidence, uncertainty, and recommended_action.
22:00 worker exits with status 1. No preceding application logs, resource metrics, or dependency checks were retained. Summarize without guessing a cause.

description: Reject memory exhaustion when ample memory remained.

messages.content: Return only JSON with assessment and reason.
Claim: memory exhaustion caused the error. Evidence: peak RSS 300 MiB, 12 GiB available, error says connection refused. assessment is supported, unsupported, or uncertain.

description: Accept a claim directly supported by a missing setting error.

messages.content: Return only JSON with assessment and reason.
Claim: startup failed because API_REGION was missing. Evidence: startup log states required setting API_REGION is missing, then exits. assessment is supported, unsupported, or uncertain.

description: Mark a network attribution uncertain without network evidence.

messages.content: Return only JSON with assessment and reason.

Claim: packet loss caused the latency spike. Evidence: only end-to-end latency was recorded; packet loss, DNS, connection, CPU, and queue metrics are unavailable. assessment is supported, unsupported, or uncertain.

description: Reject a dependency-failure claim when the dependency succeeded.

messages.content: Return only JSON with assessment and reason.

Claim: the database was unavailable. Evidence: the database query returned 200 rows in 24 ms; the next log reports local output serialization failed. assessment is supported, unsupported, or uncertain.

description: Accept resource exhaustion supported by direct queue metrics.

messages.content: Return only JSON with assessment and reason.
Claim: the bounded work queue was exhausted. Evidence: capacity 500, depth 500, enqueue returned queue_full, and dependencies remained healthy. assessment is supported, unsupported, or uncertain.

description: Inspect latency using two read-only tools.

messages.content: Return only JSON with tool_calls; use only listed tools.
Investigate a latency spike. Available tools: inspect_metrics(service), read_recent_logs(service), create_operator_ticket(summary). Plan at most two calls without executing them.

description: Inspect a dependency failure without inventing repair tools.

messages.content: Return only JSON with tool_calls; use only listed tools.
Investigate connection refusals. Available tools: check_dependency_health(name), read_recent_logs(service), create_operator_ticket(summary). Plan one or two calls; do not perform repairs.

description: Gather configuration provenance with approved tools.

messages.content: Return only JSON with tool_calls; use only listed tools.
A setting changed unexpectedly. Available tools: read_config_metadata(service), compare_release_manifest(release), create_operator_ticket(summary). Plan at most two read-only calls.

description: Assess capacity using read-only queue and worker tools.

messages.content: Return only JSON with tool_calls; use only listed tools.
A bounded queue is full. Available tools: inspect_queue_metrics(queue), inspect_worker_health(pool), create_operator_ticket(summary). Plan at most two calls and do not purge data.

description: Escalate ambiguous evidence through an available ticket tool.

messages.content: Return only JSON with tool_calls; use only listed tools.
Evidence remains ambiguous after local checks. Available tools: read_recent_logs(service), inspect_metrics(service), create_operator_ticket(summary). Plan exactly one call to request human review.
